premise: I sold my paid-off condo and used the $90,000 to buy land and build a tiny-house eco-retreat for my daughter and son-in-law, taking a secured promissory note and a written promise I could live in the guest cabin; they secretly sold the whole property for $410,000, pocketed the cash, and vanished to Costa Rica. I foreclosed on the note, reclaimed the land at the same sale price because the sale was voided, and now run a luxury glamping site called ‘Second Chance Acres’ — I garnish their stateside bank accounts monthly for the restitution, and every new booking triggers an automatic email receipt sent directly to their inbox.
